title: Agropyron pectiniforme
intro: Agropyron pectiniforme, also known as Elymus pectiniformis, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in treating conditions such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Agropyron pectiniforme include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Agropyron pectiniforme is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal soups and granules.
